metadata.reasoning_chain: The time constant of this circuit is $\tau=R C=1 \times 10^{3} \times 1 \times 10^{-6}=10^{-3}\,\mathrm{s}=1\,\mathrm{ms}$. The voltages at A and B are $V_{\mathrm{A}}=5 u(t)-5 u(t-1)$, $V_{\mathrm{B}}=5 e^{-t/\tau} u(t)-5 e^{-(t-1)/\tau} u(t-1)=5 e^{-t} u(t)-5 e^{-(t-1)} u(t-1)$, where $t$ is in ms. The time curve of $V_{B}$ is shown in Fig. 3.16.

However, the question explicitly depends on Fig. 3.15 for the pulse definition, and that figure is not present, so key input details are missing even though the expected answer format is still identifiable.
